title: Internet Radio on Your PowerMac G4
description: Find live streams, import .m3u playlists into iTunes, and enjoy endless music on your classic Mac.
author: G4 Audio Team
content:
Why Internet Radio on a G4?

The PowerMac G4 makes a perfect networked jukebox. iTunes 9 and earlier have native support for streaming audio via .m3u and .pls files.

Finding Streams

Visit internet-radio.com on any machine. Browse genres and look for streams ending in .m3u or .pls. Right-click and save the playlist file.

Importing into iTunes

Transfer the .m3u file to your G4 via USB, network share, or even floppy. Double-click it. iTunes will open and start streaming immediately. To save it, drag the station into a playlist.

Recommended Stations for G4

  • Ambient Sleeping Pill - 64k stream, easy on the CPU
  • Radio Paradise - eclectic mix
  • SomaFM Groove Salad - classic downtempo

Troubleshooting

If a stream won't load, lower the buffer size in iTunes preferences > Playback. Some streams require QuickTime 7 to be installed.
